goal_title_suggestions: Grow My Online Store to 6 Figures

variants: Write 3 ad variations. Each one highlights a different product benefit.
Create 3 ad versions. Test a different angle in each one.
Draft 3 ad options. Cover price, emotion, and feature in one each.
tasks.why: Day 1 aha — instant ad variations ready to run

variants: Go to your Shopify or platform dashboard and list your top 5 products by revenue over the last 30 days — write the product name, units sold, and revenue for each.
Open your store admin, sort products by revenue for the last 30 days, and record the top 5: name, units sold, and total revenue per product.
Pull your store's revenue report right now and document your 5 best-selling products with their unit counts and revenue — these are your growth levers.
tasks.why: Knowing your winners tells you where to double down with ads, content, and optimization.

variants: Film a raw 30-second TikTok of your #1 product in use — no script, no editing, just show it working — and post it now with 3 relevant hashtags.
Pick up your phone, shoot a 30-second TikTok showing your best product doing its job, and post it immediately with 3 niche hashtags — no perfection, just publish.
Record and post a 30-second TikTok of your top product in action right now — unscripted, unfiltered — add 3 hashtags and hit publish before you overthink it.
tasks.why: Organic TikTok content builds social proof and drives free traffic from day one.

variants: Rewrite your top product description. Make it punchier and benefit-led.
Refresh a product description. Cut fluff, add a strong benefit line.
Update your best product copy. Lead with the outcome, not the specs.
tasks.why: Day 2 aha — rewritten description that converts better

variants: Upload your customer email CSV to Meta Ads Manager as a Custom Audience — name it 'Buyers [Month Year]' and let it process.
In Meta Ads Manager, create a new Custom Audience from your customer email file — label it clearly with today's month and year so you can track it.
Take the customer CSV you prepared and upload it into Meta Ads Manager as a Custom Audience right now — give it a dated name so it's easy to find later.
tasks.why: This audience becomes the seed for your lookalike — the most cost-efficient cold audience available.

variants: Film and post a second TikTok today showing a different angle of your product — this time focus on the problem it solves, not the product itself — post with 3 hashtags.
Shoot and publish a TikTok today from the customer's perspective: lead with the problem your product fixes, then show the product as the solution — 3 hashtags, post now.
Record a new TikTok today that opens with the pain point your product eliminates, then reveals the product — keep it under 45 seconds, add 3 hashtags, publish immediately.
tasks.why: Problem-first content hooks viewers faster and drives higher intent traffic to your store.

variants: Open Meta Ads Manager and create a Lookalike Audience from your 'Buyers' Custom Audience — set it to 1% lookalike in your primary country and save it.
In Meta Ads Manager, build a 1% Lookalike Audience from your customer Custom Audience right now — select your primary country and name it 'LLA 1% Buyers'.
Go to Meta Ads Manager, find your buyer Custom Audience, and generate a 1% Lookalike Audience from it targeting your main market — do it now, it takes 2 minutes.
tasks.why: A 1% lookalike mirrors your best customers — it's the most targeted cold audience you can run ads to.

variants: Set up a new Meta ad campaign with a $10/day budget targeting your 1% Lookalike Audience — use your best existing product photo as the creative and write a 1-line hook in the headline.
In Meta Ads Manager, create a new campaign: objective = Sales, budget = $10/day, audience = your LLA 1% Buyers, creative = your strongest product image, and a punchy 1-sentence headline — launch it.
Build and launch a $10/day Meta campaign right now: target the 1% lookalike you just created, use your top product photo, write a direct headline, and hit publish.
tasks.why: Ten dollars a day against your best audience is enough data to start learning what converts.

variants: Film and post your third TikTok this week — this time do a 'what I ordered vs. what I got' or unboxing format showing your product arriving and in use — post with 3 hashtags.
Record a TikTok today using an unboxing or reveal format: show the package, the product, and it in use in one continuous clip — add 3 hashtags and post it now.
Post your third TikTok of the week: shoot an unboxing-style video where you open the product and immediately demonstrate it — 3 hashtags, upload and publish today.
tasks.why: Three videos in 3 days trains the TikTok algorithm to distribute your content more broadly.

variants: Check your Meta Ads Manager for yesterday's campaign data — write down impressions, CPM, and any clicks or add-to-carts in your audit doc.
Open Meta Ads Manager right now and log yesterday's ad metrics — impressions, CPM, link clicks — in your running audit doc.
Pull yesterday's Meta campaign results: note the impression count, CPM, and click-through rate in your audit doc — takes 2 minutes.
tasks.why: Daily check-ins in week one help you spot problems before they waste budget.

variants: Open your store's top product page and read the title, first paragraph of copy, and bullet points out loud — note any vague or generic lines you'll fix later this week.
Go to your best-selling product page, read the headline and top 3 bullet points aloud, and write down the weakest line in your audit doc — you'll rewrite it on day 6.
Pull up the product page for your #1 seller and read the headline and opening copy critically — mark the single weakest sentence in your audit notes for revision.
tasks.why: Most product pages bleed conversion on the first scroll — you need to know exactly what's weak before you fix it.

variants: Open your store's traffic source breakdown in Google Analytics for the last 30 days and write down the top 3 channels with their session counts and conversion rates by channel.
In Google Analytics, pull the Acquisition report for the past 30 days — record your top 3 traffic sources with session volume and conversion rate each in your audit doc.
Go to Google Analytics acquisition report right now and document which 3 channels sent the most sessions in the last month, plus the conversion rate for each — log it in your doc.
tasks.why: Knowing which channels convert lets you invest more where it's already working.

variants: Write a promo email. Announce a sale with a clear call to action.
Draft a promotional email. Short, punchy, with one offer and one CTA.
Create a sale email. Lead with the deal, close with a buy button.
tasks.why: Promo emails drive immediate revenue

variants: In Klaviyo, locate the pre-built 'Abandoned Cart' flow template and activate it with default settings — you can refine the copy later, but it needs to be on today.
Find the Abandoned Cart flow in Klaviyo's flow library, enable it using the default template, and confirm it's active — don't wait to customize, just turn it on.
Turn on Klaviyo's Abandoned Cart flow right now using the default template — an imperfect flow running today beats a perfect one you'll set up next month.
tasks.why: Abandoned cart emails recover 5-15% of lost sales passively — every day it's off is money left on the table.

variants: Post a TikTok today showing your product being used by a real person — ask a friend, family member, or film yourself using it naturally — add context in the caption about who it's for, post with 3 hashtags.
Film and post a TikTok where someone actually uses your product on camera — borrow a person if needed — write a caption that names exactly who the product is made for, then publish with 3 hashtags.
Record a TikTok featuring your product in real use — show a human being benefiting from it — add a caption that targets your ideal customer directly, post now with 3 hashtags.
tasks.why: Social proof through real product use outperforms solo product shots in engagement and conversion.

variants: Rewrite the weakest line you flagged on day 4 on your top product page — make it specific, benefit-driven, and under 15 words — save and publish the change now.
Open your top product page, find the weak line you noted on day 4, rewrite it to be concrete and benefit-focused in under 15 words, and publish the edit.
Go back to the weak sentence you caught on day 4's product page audit — rewrite it now to be direct and specific, under 15 words — push the change live today.
tasks.why: Even one stronger line on a high-traffic product page can move your conversion rate measurably.

variants: Check the current photos on your top product page — identify the one weakest image (blurry, poorly lit, low-context) and replace it with a better photo or flag it to reshoot this week.
Audit the product images on your #1 seller: find the single worst photo and either swap it for a better file right now or write 'reshoot' next to it in your audit doc with a deadline of 3 days.
Pull up your top product page and evaluate every image — mark the weakest one and either replace it today or schedule a reshoot within 3 days — no indefinite 'eventually'.
tasks.why: Product photos are the biggest visual conversion driver — one bad image costs you sales every hour.

variants: Film and post today's TikTok — this time respond to a common objection or question you get from customers: pick one real question, answer it on camera while showing the product, and post with 3 hashtags.
Record a TikTok that answers the #1 customer objection you hear — speak directly to the doubt, show the product resolving it, and post it with 3 hashtags today.
Post a TikTok today built around a real FAQ: state the question in the first 2 seconds, answer it visually using the product, publish with 3 hashtags — this is your most valuable content format.
tasks.why: Objection-busting content converts skeptical buyers who are close to purchasing but need reassurance.

variants: Pull up your Meta Ads Manager and write down this week's key stats: total spend, CPM, CTR, and any add-to-carts or purchases — note them in your audit doc.
Open Meta Ads Manager and record your week-one ad summary: spend, CPM, click-through rate, and purchases — log the numbers in your audit doc right now.
Check Meta Ads Manager for your week-one summary — write down spend, CPM, CTR, and purchase events — you need these numbers to benchmark against week two.
tasks.why: Week-one ad data is your baseline — everything you optimize against starts here.

variants: Open your TikTok analytics and record the views, profile visits, and link clicks for each of this week's videos — write them down next to each video title in your audit doc.
Go to TikTok Studio analytics right now and log the view count, profile visits, and link clicks for every video you posted this week — organize them by day.
Pull TikTok analytics for your 5 posts this week and document views, profile visits, and link clicks per video — you need this to know which format to repeat.
tasks.why: Identifying which TikTok format got the most traction tells you exactly what to post more of.

variants: Review everything in your audit doc from days 1-6: conversion rate, top products, traffic sources, ad results, and TikTok metrics — write one sentence on what's working and one on what needs fixing next week.

Open your audit doc and do a full week-one review: read your baseline numbers, TikTok performance, and ad results — then write 2 sentences: what's the clear win this week, and what's the one thing you'll change next week.

Do your first weekly review now: scan your audit doc from the past 6 days, look at every number you've written, and write a single 'keep doing this' and a single 'fix this next week' — be specific, no vague observations.
tasks.why: Weekly reviews stop you from executing blindly — they force decisions that compound over 90 days.

variants: Write an abandoned cart email. Recover shoppers who left items behind.
Draft a cart recovery email. Remind buyers what they almost bought.
Create a cart-abandonment message. Use urgency to bring buyers back.
tasks.why: Cart recovery emails reclaim lost sales

variants: Open the first email in your Welcome Series flow and rewrite the subject line to be benefit-specific and under 8 words — save the change.
Go into your Klaviyo Welcome Series and update the subject line of email #1 — make it specific to what the subscriber gets, not generic like 'Welcome to our store' — save it.
Find the first email in your Welcome Series flow and change its subject line to something that names a specific benefit — under 8 words, no generic greetings — publish the update.
tasks.why: The subject line determines open rate — a generic welcome subject loses half your new subscribers immediately.

variants: Post today's TikTok — replicate the format from your highest-performing video this week (check the analytics you logged on day 7) but with a different product or use case — post with 3 hashtags.

Look at your day-7 TikTok analytics, identify your top-performing video format, and film a new video using the same structure with a different angle or product application — post with 3 hashtags today.
Use your week-one TikTok data to identify your best-performing video format, then replicate that structure for today's post with new content — upload and publish with 3 hashtags.
tasks.why: Repeating what the algorithm already rewarded is faster than guessing with new formats.

variants: In Klaviyo, activate the 'Post-Purchase' flow — use the default template and confirm it triggers after a completed order.
Go to Klaviyo and turn on the Post-Purchase flow right now — make sure it fires after an order is marked complete in your store.
Enable Klaviyo's Post-Purchase flow today using the built-in template — verify the trigger is set to 'Placed Order' so it fires immediately after a sale.
tasks.why: Post-purchase emails that ask for reviews and upsell related products increase LTV without additional ad spend.

variants: Open your store's Shopify admin and check your average order value for the last 30 days — then identify the one product most likely to be added as a bundle with your top seller and note it in your audit doc.
Pull your AOV from Shopify's reports for the last 30 days, then look at your product catalog and write down the single best candidate for a bundle with your #1 product — log it in your doc.
Check your average order value in your store dashboard for the last 30 days, then scan your catalog for the product most complementary to your top seller — note the bundle idea in your audit doc.
tasks.why: Bundling two complementary products is the fastest way to increase AOV without a price increase.

variants: Post a TikTok today featuring your product solving a specific frustration — name the frustration in the first 3 words of the video, show the product fix it in the next 20 seconds, add 3 hashtags.
Film a TikTok that opens with a named frustration in the first 3 seconds — 'Tired of [specific problem]?' — then demonstrate your product ending that frustration — post with 3 hashtags now.
Record and post a TikTok: lead with a pain point stated plainly in the first 3 words, let the product be the resolution, keep it under 45 seconds, and publish with 3 hashtags today.
tasks.why: Pain-point hooks generate higher save rates, which tells TikTok to distribute the video more broadly.

variants: Log into Meta Ads Manager and write down today's cost per click and cost per purchase — compare it against your week-one average from your audit doc.
Open Meta Ads Manager, check today's CPC and cost per purchase, and note whether they're above or below your week-one average in your audit doc.
Pull your Meta campaign's current CPC and CPP from Ads Manager and record them in your audit doc — mark if they're trending up or down from your day-7 numbers.
tasks.why: Tracking CPC and CPP daily in week two shows whether your audience is warming up or burning out.

variants: Go to your store's analytics and find your bounce rate on the top product page — if it's above 60%, note 'high bounce — needs fix' in your audit doc and write one specific hypothesis for why.
Check your product page bounce rate in Google Analytics — if it's over 60%, log it in your audit doc and write one specific reason it might be happening (slow load, weak headline, bad photo).

Open Google Analytics, find the bounce rate for your top product page, and if it exceeds 60%, add it to your audit doc with one concrete theory for the cause — not 'maybe the page' but a specific element.
tasks.why: A high bounce rate on your best product page is a conversion killer you can systematically fix.

variants: Post today's TikTok — this time show a before-and-after transformation that your product enables: show the 'before' state in the first 5 seconds, transition to 'after', publish with 3 hashtags.
Film and post a before-and-after TikTok: open on the problem state clearly, cut to the solution state your product creates, keep it under 40 seconds, and post with 3 hashtags.
Record a TikTok today using the before/after format — first 5 seconds = the struggle, next 20 seconds = the result your product delivers — upload with 3 hashtags and publish now.
tasks.why: Before-and-after content is the highest-performing e-commerce TikTok format for product demonstration.

variants: Write a bundle idea. Pick 2 products that pair well together.
Create a product bundle. Combine two items and name the set.
Draft a bundle offer. Choose a pairing and write the pitch line.
tasks.why: Bundles increase average order value

variants: Go to your top product page and count the total number of customer reviews displayed — if it's under 10 visible reviews, send a personal email to your last 20 buyers asking for a review with a direct link.

Check how many reviews are showing on your #1 product page right now — if fewer than 10 are visible, draft and send a personal email to your 20 most recent customers asking for a review, include the direct review link.

Open your top product page and count the reviews — if there are fewer than 10 showing, write and send a direct email today to your last 20 buyers asking for a review and include the link to leave one.
tasks.why: Review count is one of the top conversion signals — every review you earn now pays dividends for months.

variants: Post a TikTok today where you show 3 different ways to use your product — keep each demo under 10 seconds, add a text overlay naming each use — publish with 3 hashtags.
Film and post a '3 ways to use [product]' TikTok: each use case gets under 10 seconds of screen time with a text label — compact, fast-paced, and post with 3 hashtags today.
Record a TikTok today showing 3 distinct uses for your product — 10 seconds each, labeled on screen — upload and publish with 3 hashtags, this format gets high save rates.
tasks.why: Multi-use content increases perceived value and reaches customers with different needs in one video.

variants: Open your Meta ad campaign and check if any ad sets have a frequency above 3.0 — if so, note it in your audit doc as 'audience fatigue risk'.
In Meta Ads Manager, check your ad frequency metric — if it's above 3.0 for any ad set, flag it in your audit doc and make a note to test a new creative tomorrow.
Pull up Meta Ads Manager and look at the frequency column for your running ad sets — anything above 3.0 gets logged in your audit doc as a creative refresh signal.
tasks.why: High ad frequency burns your audience — catching it early saves budget before performance tanks.

variants: Open Canva and design one new ad creative for Meta using a real customer quote or review as the visual centerpiece — export it as a 1:1 and 9:16 version and save both to your desktop.
In Canva, build a new ad graphic that leads with a genuine customer review — make a square (1:1) and vertical (9:16) version — export both and save them for upload.
Use Canva right now to create a new Meta ad image built around a real review or testimonial — export it in square and vertical formats — you'll upload these tomorrow.
tasks.why: Testimonial-based creatives consistently outperform product-only images in cold traffic Meta campaigns.

variants: Post a TikTok today doing a live 'pack an order' video — show the actual fulfillment process, add a caption about how many orders you've shipped, and publish with 3 hashtags.
Film yourself packing or fulfilling a real order and post it on TikTok today — mention in the caption how many total orders you've sent — this builds trust, publish with 3 hashtags.
Record and post a TikTok of your order fulfillment in action: show the packing, the product, the care you take — add the total order count in your caption — post with 3 hashtags now.
tasks.why: Behind-the-scenes fulfillment content builds brand credibility and drives impulse purchases.

variants: Upload the new ad creatives you made on day 12 into your existing Meta campaign as a new ad — keep the same audience and budget, just test the new visual.
Take the Canva creatives from day 12, upload them into your Meta Ads Manager campaign as a new ad within the same ad set — don't change the targeting, just the creative.
Add yesterday's new ad creatives to your live Meta campaign right now — same ad set, same budget, new image — this is your first creative split test.
tasks.why: Running two creatives against the same audience lets Meta find the better performer automatically.

variants: Open your store and check your top product page load time using Google PageSpeed Insights — enter the URL, run the test, and write down the mobile performance score in your audit doc.
Go to Google PageSpeed Insights right now, paste your top product page URL, run the mobile test, and record the score in your audit doc — anything under 50 is a red flag.
Run your top product page through Google PageSpeed Insights on mobile right now — write the score in your audit doc and note the #1 suggested fix it gives you.
tasks.why: Page load time is a direct conversion factor — a 1-second delay can cut mobile conversions by 20%.

variants: Post a TikTok today where you address a 1-star review or common complaint honestly — explain what changed or why it happened — don't delete bad feedback, answer it publicly.
Film and post a TikTok responding to a real piece of critical feedback about your product — be honest, be direct, explain what you've done about it — publish with 3 hashtags.
Record a TikTok today that responds to a real customer complaint or negative review: name the issue, explain the fix, and show the product — post with 3 hashtags, this builds massive trust.
tasks.why: Addressing criticism publicly is one of the highest-trust content moves an e-commerce brand can make.

variants: Write an SEO meta description. One sentence for your top product page.
Draft SEO meta copy. A short line that gets clicks from search results.
Create SEO descriptions. Write one for each of your 3 top pages.
tasks.why: SEO meta copy improves click-through rates

variants: Review your TikTok analytics for the full two weeks — list your top 3 videos by views and note what format each one used (before/after, FAQ, demo, etc.) in your audit doc.
Open TikTok Studio, look at your 14-day video performance, and list your 3 most-viewed videos with their format type — write this in your audit doc so you know what to produce more of.
Pull 14-day TikTok analytics right now and rank your top 3 videos by views — note the content format for each one in your audit doc — this is your content playbook for the next 30 days.
tasks.why: Two weeks of data reveals your winning content format — executing more of it compounds your reach.

variants: Do your two-week business review: open your audit doc, check every metric you've tracked since day 1 — conversion rate, top products, ad performance, TikTok, email flows — write 3 specific improvements you'll make in week 3.
Open your full audit doc and review all two weeks of data: GA numbers, ad metrics, TikTok performance, and Klaviyo flow status — write down 3 concrete changes you'll execute in days 15-21.

Complete your 2-week review now by going through your entire audit doc — every metric, every note — and write 3 specific actions you'll take differently in the next 7 days based on what the data shows.
tasks.why: The two-week mark is when most people drift — a written decision list keeps you executing, not just watching.

variants: Open Klaviyo and check the open rate and click rate on your Welcome Series email #1 — write the numbers in your audit doc and note if the open rate is above or below 40%.
In Klaviyo, pull the stats for your Welcome Series first email — record the open rate and click rate in your audit doc and flag it if open rate is under 40%.
Go to Klaviyo analytics for your Welcome Series right now and log email #1's open rate and click rate — 40% open rate is your minimum target, note whether you're hitting it.
tasks.why: Welcome email open rates above 40% confirm your subject line and sender reputation are strong.

variants: Rewrite the subject line of your Abandoned Cart email #1 in Klaviyo — make it specific to the product left behind using a merge tag, not generic — save and publish.
Open Klaviyo's Abandoned Cart flow, find the first email, and update its subject line to reference the specific product abandoned using a personalization merge tag — publish the change.
Go into your Abandoned Cart flow in Klaviyo and change email #1's subject line to include the product name dynamically — use the correct merge tag, save it, and confirm it's live.
tasks.why: A personalized abandoned cart subject line increases recovery rate by making the email feel direct, not automated.

variants: Post today's TikTok using the top-performing format from your day-14 analysis — replicate the structure that got the most views, apply it to a new product angle, and post with 3 hashtags.
Use the winning TikTok format you identified on day 14 and film a new video using that exact structure with fresh content — publish with 3 hashtags today, don't deviate from what's working.
Film and post a TikTok today built on your proven winning format from the day-14 review — same structure, different content angle — add 3 hashtags and publish it now.
tasks.why: Doubling down on proven formats is how you systematically grow your TikTok reach week over week.

variants: Open your store admin and pull up the product pages for your top 3 sellers — check if each has at least 5 bullet points and a minimum of 4 photos — log what's missing in your audit doc.
Go through your top 3 product pages right now and confirm each has 5+ bullet points and 4+ photos — write down what's missing for each product in your audit doc.
Check your 3 best-selling product pages one by one: count bullet points and photos for each — log any page with fewer than 5 bullets or 4 photos in your audit doc as needing work.
tasks.why: Product pages with thin copy and few images have lower conversion — you need to know exactly which ones are underbuilt.

variants: Pick the product page with the biggest content gap from your audit and write 3 new benefit-focused bullet points for it — publish the changes today.
Find the weakest product page from your day-16 audit and add 3 new bullet points to it right now — each bullet names a specific benefit, not a feature — publish them.
Take the most underbuilt product page from your audit and write 3 more bullet points: lead each with the benefit, not the spec — add them to the page and go live today.
tasks.why: More benefit-focused bullet points increase the chance a customer finds the specific reason they'll buy.

variants: Post a TikTok today featuring a real customer review as your hook — read it aloud, show the product, then add your own commentary on why customers feel that way — post with 3 hashtags.
Film a TikTok where you read a genuine customer review on camera and then demonstrate the product feature or outcome the review mentions — post with 3 hashtags today.
Record and post a TikTok today: open by reading a real 5-star review aloud, visually back it up with the product in use, then publish with 3 hashtags — social proof on video converts.
tasks.why: Customer review content on TikTok combines social proof with organic distribution — it's both an ad and credibility signal.

variants: Write a pop-up offer. Short message with a discount for first visitors.
Draft pop-up copy. A quick deal that catches new visitors' attention.
Create a site pop-up. Lead with the offer, keep it under 2 lines.
tasks.why: Pop-up offers convert first-time visitors to subscribers

variants: Open Klaviyo and check your Abandoned Cart flow's recovery rate — write down how many carts were abandoned, how many emails sent, and how many converted in your audit doc.
In Klaviyo analytics, find your Abandoned Cart flow stats: total triggered, emails delivered, and revenue recovered — log all three numbers in your audit doc.
Go to Klaviyo, open your Abandoned Cart flow analytics, and record total triggers, email open rate, and recovered orders — these numbers show whether the flow is working.
tasks.why: If your abandoned cart recovery rate is under 5%, the copy or timing needs adjustment — you need the data first.

variants: Post a TikTok today featuring a comparison — your product versus the alternative people use before they find you — be direct, show both, explain why yours wins — post with 3 hashtags.
Film and post a TikTok that compares your product head-to-head with the common alternative: show both, state the difference clearly, and don't be shy about winning the comparison — 3 hashtags.
Record a TikTok today using a comparison format: show what people normally use versus your product, demonstrate the gap, and post with 3 hashtags — this is your most persuasive selling content.
tasks.why: Comparison content captures buyers who are already evaluating solutions — your highest-intent audience.

variants: Open the body copy of your Abandoned Cart email #1 in Klaviyo and rewrite the first sentence — make it specific to the cart abandonment, not a generic 'you forgot something' message.
Go into Klaviyo's Abandoned Cart flow and update the first line of email #1's body — make it direct and specific about the product left behind, not a template opener — save it.
Edit the opening line of your Abandoned Cart email #1 in Klaviyo right now — it should call out the specific product left in the cart, not use a boilerplate intro — publish the change.
tasks.why: The first sentence of a recovery email determines whether it reads like a personal note or a mass blast.

variants: Go to your store admin and check if you have any upsell or cross-sell app installed — if not, install Frequently Bought Together or a native Shopify upsell app and configure one product pairing.
Open your Shopify app store and confirm whether you have an upsell or cross-sell app active — if not, install one today and set up one product pairing on your top seller.
Check your Shopify app list for an active upsell tool — if you don't have one, install a cross-sell app right now and set up a single 'often bought together' pairing on your #1 product.
tasks.why: An in-cart upsell is the fastest lever to increase average order value without new traffic.

variants: Post a TikTok today showing your product being gifted or given as a present — film the reaction, the reveal, or the packaging — use gift-giving language in the caption, post with 3 hashtags.
Film and post a TikTok today framing your product as a gift: show it being wrapped, given, or reacted to — add gift-occasion language in the caption and publish with 3 hashtags.
Record a TikTok today centered on your product as a gift idea: show the packaging, the giving, or a reaction — write a caption that names a specific occasion — post with 3 hashtags.
tasks.why: Gift-framing content expands your buyer pool beyond direct users to anyone shopping for others.

variants: Open your Shopify analytics and find your cart abandonment rate for the last 30 days — write it down in your audit doc next to your Klaviyo recovery rate from day 17.
Go to Shopify analytics, pull the checkout funnel report, and record your cart abandonment rate — add it to your audit doc next to the Klaviyo recovery data you logged on day 17.
Check your Shopify checkout funnel analytics right now and log your cart abandonment rate in your audit doc — compare it to the recovery rate from your Klaviyo data on day 17.
tasks.why: Knowing abandonment rate alongside recovery rate tells you the real dollar gap your email flow needs to close.

variants: Add a second email to your Klaviyo Abandoned Cart flow — set it to send 24 hours after email #1, include a specific product benefit the customer might have missed, and save it.
Open your Abandoned Cart flow in Klaviyo and add a second email step: send 24 hours after the first, body should highlight one specific benefit or feature not mentioned in email #1 — activate it.

Go to Klaviyo's Abandoned Cart flow and create a second email 24 hours after the first — this email should add new information (a benefit, a review, a guarantee) and include a clear CTA — save and publish.
tasks.why: A two-email abandoned cart sequence consistently outperforms a single-email approach in recovery revenue.

variants: Post a TikTok today where you answer the #2 most common question you get about your product — not the top one you've already covered — answer it on camera while showing the product.
Film and post a TikTok answering your second most-asked customer question — state the question in the first 3 seconds, answer it clearly while showing the product, post with 3 hashtags.
Record and post a TikTok today built around your #2 FAQ: open with the question on screen, answer it while demonstrating the product, and publish with 3 hashtags — keep it under 40 seconds.
tasks.why: Answering FAQs on TikTok positions you as the authority and captures buyers who need reassurance before purchasing.

variants: Write a reactivation email. Reach out to buyers who haven't purchased lately.
Draft a win-back email. Give lapsed customers a reason to come back.
Create a reactivation offer. One email with a deal for quiet customers.
tasks.why: Reactivation emails recover churned customers

variants: Add the new Canva creatives to your Meta campaign as a third ad — same audience, same budget split — let all three run for 48 hours before comparing.
